name: LatLng

aliases.name: Coord
aliases.comment:
Split coordinate component parts
Sign is either -1 or 1 and all other components are positive
aliases.type: { sign : Basics.Int, deg : Basics.Int, min : Basics.Int, sec : Basics.Int, ms : Basics.Int }

values.name: by
values.comment: Move location by given lat and lng angles

values.name: center
values.comment: Computes the center point of the polyline

values.name: chain
values.comment:
Make a chain of transformations in the given point

Each transformation is typically an operation like `moveNorth (meters 5)`

Return a list of partial applications.

Example:

    chain loc
        [ move (meters 60) (meters 10)
        , northByCoords 0 0 1 256
        , westBy (Angle.degrees 180)
        ]

values.name: coord
values.comment: Extract coordinate parts from float

values.name: coordToString
values.comment:
Format a decimal latitude or longitude coordinate

    coordToString -45.5 "N" "S" ==> "45°30'00.00\"S"

values.name: decodePair
values.comment: Decode location from a string with a pair of floats

values.name: decodePretty
values.comment: Decode LatLng from a prettified representation in a JSON string

values.name: decodeTuple
values.comment: Decode location from a string with a pair of floats

values.name: decoder
values.comment: Decode LatLng from a known format

values.name: distance
values.comment:
Distance between two points, in meters.

Uses the great circle distance (<https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great-circle_distance>)

This approximates the Earth by a sphere, which in most occasions is good enough.

values.name: eastBy
values.comment: Move point east by given longitude angle

values.name: eastByCoords
values.comment: Move point east by given longitude cooordinates (in degrees, minutes, seconds, milliseconds)
values.type: Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> LatLng.LatLng -> LatLng.LatLng

values.name: encoder
values.comment: Encode LatLng as a JSON string

values.name: fromLatLng
values.comment: Create location from lat/lng values

values.name: fromLatLngDegrees
values.comment: Create location from lat/lng values

values.name: googleMapsUrl
values.comment: Google Maps URL associated with the given location

values.name: latitude
values.comment: Return location\`s latitude

values.name: longitude
values.comment: Return location\`s longitude

values.name: move
values.comment: Move point by (x, y) distance

values.name: moveEast
values.comment: Move point east by given distance

values.name: moveNorth
values.comment: Move point north by given distance

values.name: moveSouth
values.comment: Move point south by given distance

values.name: moveWest
values.comment: Move point west by given distance

values.name: northBy
values.comment: Move point north by given latitude angle

values.name: northByCoords
values.comment: Move point south by given latitude cooordinates (in degrees, minutes, seconds, milliseconds)
values.type: Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> LatLng.LatLng -> LatLng.LatLng

values.name: parse
values.comment: Parse a pretty string of Lat/Lon data

values.name: parseCoord
values.comment:
Parse a single coordinate with the given pair of directions

This can be used to specify the N-S or E-W directions or localized
versions of those symbols

Example
parseCoord ("L", "O") "45°10'50.12L"
==> Coord 1 45 10 50 120

values.name: parsePair
values.comment: Convert string with a pair of floats to a LatLng pair

values.name: pathFrom
values.comment:
Create linear path starting at the given LatLng point

The conversion assumes the earth is locally flat, i.e., it gives
good results for nearby points and poor conversions if distances
are comensuarable to Earth radius.

values.name: pathFromCenter
values.comment: Convert Polyline into path, taking the center point as the origin.

values.name: pathFromStart
values.comment:
Convert Polyline into path

Works like pathFrom, but uses the first element of list as the origin reference

values.name: southBy
values.comment: Move point south by given latitude angle

values.name: southByCoords
values.comment: Move point south by given latitude cooordinates (in degrees, minutes, seconds, milliseconds)
values.type: Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> LatLng.LatLng -> LatLng.LatLng

values.name: toAngle
values.comment: Converts Coord to float

values.name: toString
values.comment: Render lat/lng as a pretty printed string

values.name: toTupleDegrees
values.comment: Convert LatLng to a tuple of floats

values.name: westBy
values.comment: Move point west by given longitude angle

values.name: westByCoords
values.comment: Move point west by given longitude cooordinates (in degrees, minutes, seconds, milliseconds)
values.type: Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> LatLng.LatLng -> LatLng.LatLng

name: Places

aliases.type: { center : LatLng.LatLng, north : LatLng.LatLng, south : LatLng.LatLng, east : LatLng.LatLng, west : LatLng.LatLng, capital : LatLng.LatLng }
